Refs #14929

Delivers the adoption + observability slice of the FLIP commit-animation lane on its own branch (the mechanism itself shipped to dev inside PR #14912's approved head): `examples/dashboard/dock` adopts the two-phase FLIP contract around its commit re-projection — marker classes carry stable item identity across instance recreation, `captureFirst`/`play` wrap the refresh seam, and the addon registers via the example's `mainThreadAddons` — so a human splitter drop and an NL operation now animate identically on the reference workspace. The addon additionally emits the **`dock-animating`** host class for exactly the motion's duration — the observability signal the signed #14779 motion-contract disposition names as the ONE consumable for `observe_motion` e2e assertions, regardless of which mechanism animates.

Evidence: L2 (syntax + the identical wiring pattern live-proven on the dockdemo surface in PR #14912's whitebox journey) → L3 DELIVERED (live verification 2026-07-10: perspective switch on examples/dashboard/dock FLIP-animated all three geometry-changed panes — swarm/terminal/logs — with the dock-animating host class observed during motion and zero residual transforms after settle). Residual: the token-source amendment (durations/easing from `--dock-transition-*` instead of hardcoded defaults) is blocked on #14779's token slice and stays on the ticket.

## Deltas from ticket

- **Consumer-status amendment applied** per the signed #14779 disposition (ticket comment thread): this lane now consumes the motion contract rather than owning duration policy; the `dock-animating` emission lands here, the token consumption follows #14779's substrate slice.
